ops.title: Size-gated cat guard and per-occurrence rewrites (pack 0.6.0)
ops.body:
# Pack 0.6.0 policy shifts (capt-hook >= 9.15.0)

Three deliberate tradeoffs landed together; don't "fix" them piecemeal.

**Per-occurrence rewrites.** Every rewrite guard (cat/sed/head/find/ls/git-pager/curl-dump) splices only the qualifying command of a compound line via `rewrite_command_occurrences` — siblings, operators, redirects, and comments survive byte-for-byte. The incident this fixes: whole-line `cl.primary` rewrites silently DROPPED sibling commands (`cat a; echo x; cat b` lost the first cat and the echo). Two invariants to preserve when editing:
- The piped/redirect exemption must live in the **condition**, not only in `to()` — the primitive's zero-rewrite fallthrough blocks when `block=` is set, so a condition that matches a piped-only line converts an Allow into a Block (this exact regression shipped mid-migration and was caught by refuter; regression rows like `ls -R src | wc -l` → Allow now pin it).
- An unrewritable flood occurrence blocks the WHOLE line (`block_if` is line-grained by design) — never "rewrite what you can, run the rest raw".

**Size-gated cat lane.** Bare `cat` rewrites only an existing file over `LARGE_READ_BYTES`; small, nonexistent, `$`-carrying, and heredoc-line operands run untouched, and multi-file cats block only when stat-able operands sum past the cap. This ACCEPTS small-file dumps for usability — the pack's mission is token-heavy commands, mirroring `read_guards.py`'s `is_large` gate. The `~`-decline is gone: the guard expands `~` itself and emits the quoted absolute path (the old decline replaced an older bug where `shlex.quote` froze `~` into a dead literal).

**Loud path failures.** `ccx code read` (all three lanes: CLI/MCP/exec) expands a leading `~` textually, stats the target pre-dispatch, and exits 3 on a missing file instead of letting tilth's query-dispatch silently turn a read into a cwd-scoped content search. Directories error with an outline pointer (the MCP directory listing is gone, deliberately). Never map bare `fs.ErrNotExist` to exit 3 — only the `backend.ErrPathNotFound` sentinel.

Related: cite `plugin/hooks/cat_rewrites.py` for the lane classifiers; the incident replay row lives in its inline tests. Known deferred siblings: grep/rg + json per-occurrence migrations (backlog), tilth misclassifying existing spacey paths on the CLI read lane (backlog bug), exec-lane exit-code identity (backlog).
ops.when: Before adjusting guard-pack rewrite policy, changing protection granularity, or tuning the large-read cap threshold
